spec_match.rationale: The page now states that the IndexedDB web API does not expose the backing store, labels runs with a ChromeStatus-based rollout inference, and pairs throughput with rollback and read/write isolation checks that target the SQLite migration's reliability motivation.

interactive.rationale: The demo remains a live IndexedDB workload with configurable object count and payload size, plus context selectors, abort/isolation toggles, wipe controls, live status tiles, and persisted run history.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now promises throughput plus backend inference, and the page delivers both while explaining the direct-observability fallback.

edge_cases.rationale: The workload now verifies abort rollback, checks read/write isolation, distinguishes fresh stores from existing stores where possible, and labels private-window and rollout-flag uncertainty instead of pretending to directly inspect the backend.

references.rationale: The see-also section still links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5161589557821440 and the page also links back to the feature index.

design_system.rationale: New CSS uses the shared design-system variables, all local button background overrides also set color, and the concept remains an interactive tool rather than a static card.

summary: Re-review after the bulk write stress page was updated to separate direct backend observability from rollout inference. The page now labels fresh, existing, private, flag-enabled, and unknown cases accurately; keeps benchmark history across runs; and adds abort rollback plus read/write isolation probes, so the previous major and moderate follow-ups are closed.
